title: More trouble brewing for Mamata's TMC? Expelled MLA Ritabrata makes fresh claim
content: Expelled TMC leader Ritabrata Banerjee expressed confidence that support for his rebel faction will grow, claiming a significant number of MLAs are backing him. He also appealed to CM Suvendu Adhikari to take action against BJP leader Priyanka Tibrewal for protesting outside a fellow rebel's residence.

title: Watch: Trump takes swipe at Indian journalist, sparks racism row
content: During a White House press interaction, President Trump questioned an Indian journalist's nationality before taking her question, later stating he was "kidding." He then described Indian Prime Minister Modi as a "good friend" and expressed confidence in a trade agreement, asserting the US now benefits significantly from its economic ties with India.

title: BCCI confirms timings of India squad announcement for UK tour
content: The BCCI will announce India's squads for the Ireland and England white-ball tours, after a selection meeting on Saturday. A press conference led by Ajit Agarkar and Devajit Saikia will begin at 1 PM IST. Shreyas Iyer is expected to become T20 captain, while youngsters Tilak Varma and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi could earn major roles.

title: Meta's highest-paid employee’s 'health message' to Anthropic, OpenAI & Google
content: Meta's top AI executive, Alexandr Wang, revealed the company's strategy to challenge rivals like OpenAI and Google by focusing on health-related AI capabilities. While acknowledging current models aren't top-tier, Wang highlighted Meta's commitment to advancing AI for health applications, aiming to integrate these features into popular platforms like Facebook and Instagram.

title: ‘Ready to co-develop Su-57 with India, no restrictions’: Putin hardsells 5th-gen fighter jet
content: Russia's President Putin has offered India joint development and production of the fifth-generation Su-57 stealth fighter jet. Despite India's withdrawal from a previous joint project, Russia is open to collaboration, aiming to bolster defence ties amidst India's own AMCA project and regional security concerns.

title: ‘You simply can't’: Ashwin on why forcing Sooryavanshi into Tests may not work
content: Ravichandran Ashwin advocates for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to pursue Test cricket, despite the 15-year-old's phenomenal white-ball success. Sooryavanshi's record-breaking IPL 2026 campaign, where he won the Orange Cap with 776 runs, has sparked debate about his future format. Ashwin acknowledges the changing landscape of cricket and the difficulty in forcing a player's hand.

title: Microsoft HR head Amy Coleman to employees: I want to be transparent about how things are feeling
content: Microsoft's Chief People Officer Amy Coleman shared employee survey results, highlighting increased employee "energized" and "empowered" feelings, alongside strengths in security and inclusion. However, challenges remain in opportunities for experience broadening, productivity support, and clarity on work's organizational connection. Coleman pledged greater transparency and communication amidst intense change.

title: 'Success of reforms and hard work Indians': PM Modi hails 7.7% GDP growth in FY26
content: India's economy achieved a strong 7.7% growth in FY26. This expansion highlights the nation's inherent economic strength. Prime Minister Narendra Modi attributed this success to effective reforms and the diligent efforts of 140 crore Indians. The government remains committed to improving ease of living and doing business. This growth creates new opportunities for the country's youth.

title: Explained: How Praggnanandhaa can win Norway Chess in a three-way battle
content: Praggnanandhaa remains firmly in contention for the Norway Chess 2026 title heading into the final round. The Indian grandmaster trails leader Wesley So by just half a point, with Alireza Firouzja also in the mix. A classical victory in his final game could propel Praggnanandhaa to the top, though the outcome will also depend on results involving So and Firouzja.

title: Venus & Jupiter conjunction: Dates when Mercury will also be visible
content: Skywatchers are in for a treat as Venus and Jupiter align closely on June 9, appearing almost side-by-side in the western sky after sunset. This celestial spectacle, visible to the naked eye, will also feature Mercury from June 11, creating a rare three-planet parade in the constellation Gemini.  Binoculars will enhance the view of this stunning astronomical event.

title: Bank loses Rs 87 lakh to SIM swap fraud, HC orders BSNL to pay over Rs 55 lakh compensation
content: Karnataka High Court holds BSNL responsible for an Rs 87 lakh bank fraud, stating the telecom giant's negligence in issuing a duplicate SIM enabled cybercriminals. The court dismissed BSNL's challenge, enhancing compensation to over Rs 50 lakh plus interest, emphasizing a heightened duty of care for financial institutions.

title: 'Preferential access': Goyal says 1st phase of India-US trade deal likely by next month
content: India and the US are making rapid progress towards resolving the remaining issues in their proposed interim trade arrangement, with both countries expected to implement the first phase of the agreement by the middle of next month, Goyal said.

title: Sending ‘kiss emojis’ to another man? J&K HC refuses wife’s plea in divorce dispute
content: The Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh High Court has upheld a family court's decision to include a question about a wife allegedly sending "kiss emojis" in a divorce case. The court stated no prejudice would be caused as the husband must prove cruelty. The wife's challenge was dismissed, with the court emphasizing evidence is key.

title: India’s GDP grows at robust 7.8% in Q4 FY26; 7.7% full year growth beats estimates
content: At constant prices, real GDP for the January-March quarter is estimated to have been at Rs 87.77 lakh crore, compared with Rs 81.40 lakh crore in the corresponding period of the previous fiscal year. Nominal GDP during the quarter is estimated at Rs 94.65 lakh crore, reflecting growth of 9.1%.

title: Taylor Swift–Travis Kelce wedding rumours swirl over Blake Lively
content: Reports surrounding Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s rumored summer wedding have sparked fresh speculation about Swift’s friendship with Blake Lively. Sources claim Swift wants distance from the ongoing legal battle involving Lively and Justin Baldoni before wedding celebrations begin. Fans also noticed Lively’s absence from reported bachelorette party plans, fueling rumors that the once close friendship may now be strained behind the scenes.

title: India to build 52‑satellite surveillance grid to monitor China, Pakistan threats
content: India is rapidly building a powerful military space presence, launching a 52-satellite constellation from 2025 to 2029, with a significant portion by the private sector. This initiative, guided by a new joint doctrine, aims to enhance surveillance, secure communications, and space awareness, bolstering national security in an increasingly contested orbital domain.

title: ‘Front half of car was under a truck’: Clarke reveals terrifying crash after IPL final
content: Former Australia captain Michael Clarke was involved in a serious road accident in India shortly after the IPL 2026 final. His vehicle crashed into a semi-trailer, leaving him with bruises and the car written off. Clarke expressed gratitude for the support from locals and the BCCI following the frightening incident.

title: Rohit Sharma likely to report at CoE on June 8; Kohli doubtful for England ODIs too
content: Rohit injured his hamstring during the IPL and was picked for the ODIs, subject to fitness clearance.

title: 'Se*ualising someone without consent...: 'Janhvi Kapoor's old interview resurfaces
content: As 'Peddi' draws fire for allegedly objectifying Janhvi Kapoor's character Achiyyamma, an April interview in which the actor spoke candidly about consent, over-sexualisation, and her right to object to uncomfortable camera angles has gone viral. Janhvi's remarks made on Raj Shamani's podcast draw a clear line between sensuality she has agreed to and sexualisation imposed without her knowledge or consent, reigniting a wider conversation about the portrayal of women in Indian cinema.

title: Maple Leafs coaching search takes new turn with familiar name
content: The Toronto Maple Leafs have reportedly interviewed Dallas Eakins for their head coaching vacancy. The move adds another seasoned name to an already long list of candidates. Eakins has a background with the organization and his NHL experience with the Edmonton Oilers and Anaheim Ducks makes him an intriguing option as Toronto continues its search.

title: 'Don't want to burden top leadership': What Annamalai told BJP in his resignation letter
content: Former Tamil Nadu BJP chief K Annamalai has resigned to launch a new political movement, aiming to end "cult and dynastic" politics. Annamalai expressed a desire to change the notion of politics and connect with local aspirations, stating national parties often fail to speak the language of Tamil Nadu's people.

title: Putin plans to expand Indo-Russian trade deal to $100 bn, energy seen as main driver
content: As Russia sets its sights on elevating trade relations with India to a remarkable $100 billion, President Putin emphasized the importance of current nuclear energy projects like the Kudankulam plant. He also hinted at forthcoming collaborations in the hydrocarbons sector.

title: Will India finally get plastic banknotes? RBI's polymer currency plan explained
content: The Reserve Bank of India is considering introducing polymer currency notes, a move that could enhance durability and security. Governor Sanjay Malhotra stated the proposal is in its preliminary stages, with the central bank examining its advantages and disadvantages. This initiative aims to address the rapid deterioration of existing paper notes and combat fake currency circulation.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 78: Ranveer Singh film ends theatrical run at Rs 1149.02 crore
content: The curtain has officially fallen on the historic, record-shattering theatrical run of 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge.' On Day 78, the Ranveer Singh-led espionage epic brought in a final Rs. 0.07 crore, concluding its domestic net collection at an unprecedented Rs 1149.02 crore, missing the monumental Rs. 1150 crore milestone by a razor-thin margin of just Rs. 0.98 crore. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the blockbuster sequel has cemented its status as the highest-grossing Hindi film in domestic box office history with a staggering worldwide gross of Rs 1813.11 crore. Coinciding with the end of its theatrical run, the film's exclusive, unrated "Raw & Undekha" cut officially premiered on JioHotstar.

title: Zorawar tank: The made-in-India war machine built to dominate China on the LAC
content: India's defence industry has achieved a significant milestone with the indigenous Zorawar light tank, developed in just 19 months for high-altitude Himalayan warfare. Designed to counter China's Type 15, this 25-tonne tank boasts a 105mm gun and advanced missile capabilities.  The army plans an initial order of 59 units, with induction expected in 2027.

title: Don’t have courage to appear for NEET again, 18-year-old writes, then dies by suicide
content: A 18-year-old aspiring doctor in Nagpur died by suicide following the NEET re-examination announcement, citing immense pressure and shattered dreams after a paper leak. Her family had borrowed heavily for her coaching. Opposition leaders have linked her tragic death to systemic failures and corruption within the examination process.

title: American woman shares how a Kerala man made a 14-hour journey to return her lost debit card
content: An American traveler, India Witkin, lost her debit card at an ATM in Kerala. A local DHL employee, Krishna, found the card and drove six hours on his day off to return it. He refused payment, stating Witkin was on a budget. This act of kindness has resonated online, highlighting honesty and compassion.

title: Will fans have to pay for free FIFA World Cup 2026 tickets issued due to website glitch?
content: A ticketing error on FIFA’s website has left dozens of football fans unexpectedly receiving free tickets for the FIFA World Cup 2026. The issue occurred during the checkout process and resulted in some supporters being allocated tickets at no cost. FIFA has now confirmed the mistake and is asking the affected fans to complete payment if they want to keep their seats for the tournament.

title: Video shows effects of intense fire that forced the world's most powerful warship to return to port
content: A stunning video uncovers the alarming reality of a fire onboard the USS Gerald R. Ford, the latest pride of the US Navy, challenging previous accounts. Crew members recounted a harrowing struggle for life as fire suppression measures faltered, leading to severe destruction in living quarters and infrastructure.

title: After vice-captaincy snub, Gambhir sends 'respect situation' warning to Pant
content: Pant was removed as India's Test vice-captain when the BCCI announced the squad for the one-off Test against Afghanistan last month

title: Who is Gauri Spratt? Aamir Khan's would be third wife and the mother of six-year-old son
content: Aamir Khan is set to marry his longtime girlfriend Gauri Spratt on July 5 in what will be his third marriage. Gauri, a fashion professional and mother of a six-year-old son, has been associated with Aamir Khan Films. The two reconnected after knowing each other for 25 years.

title: As China and Pakistan move forward with their fifth generation plans, India's two short term options
content: As India grapples with a shrinking air force fleet and setbacks in its advanced fighter jet initiatives, Russia has stepped in with a compelling proposition: the Su-57 fighter jets, offered with technology transfer. This comes at a crucial time as China expands its own stealth fighter capabilities.

title: 'Nothing to worry': DKS in damage-control mode after 'closest of friend' quits cabinet
content: Karnataka chief minister DK Shivakumar has assured that there is "nothing to worry" regarding the resignation of minister Ramalinga Reddy. Reddy, a close friend and senior leader, expressed dissatisfaction with his allocated portfolio and sought a different ministerial post. Shivakumar stated he would discuss the matter with Reddy to resolve the issue.

title: 'We should end cult & dynastic politics': Annamalai quits BJP, announces new 'political movement'
content: Former BJP leader K Annamalai has launched a new political movement, the APJ Abdul Kalam Centre for Ethics and Politics, based in Coimbatore. He announced his intention to contest future Assembly elections and stressed the need for systemic political reform, aiming to end 'cult politics' and dynastic rule.

title: 'Hasn’t got fair chance': Gambhir confirms GT batter at No.3 for Afghanistan Test
content: The number three position has remained unsettled for India in Test cricket since Cheteshwar Pujara moved out of the side. Since then, seven different batters have been tried at one-drop, but none have managed to make the spot their own. Several of them were also not given an extended run in the role.

title: PM Modi welcomes AirTrunk's Rs 3 lakh crore investment plan in India
content: Blackstone-backed AirTrunk plans a massive Rs 3 lakh crore investment in India by 2030 to boost digital infrastructure, including data centres and AI capacity. Prime Minister Modi welcomed the move, highlighting its potential to strengthen India's global position in cloud computing and AI, create jobs, and drive innovation-led growth.

title: ‘If there's an opportunity ...’: Gambhir backs ICC's pink-ball rule for bad light
content: India head coach Gautam Gambhir supports the ICC's new rule allowing a pink ball under floodlights to combat bad light in Test matches. He believes this proactive measure will help ensure matches reach a conclusion, especially in crucial World Test Championship scenarios, preventing teams from being denied results due to weather.

title: What makes the S-400 India's most powerful air defence system and why it matters to Pakistan
content: India's air defence capabilities have been significantly bolstered by the S-400 system, a formidable shield against aerial threats. Its advanced radar and missile technology allows for the interception of aircraft and ballistic missiles at extreme ranges, far surpassing Western counterparts. This strategic asset provides crucial coverage against regional adversaries, enhancing national security.

title: Kharge files nomination papers for Rajya Sabha polls from Karnataka
content: Rahul Gandhi, D.K. Shivakumar, Siddaramaiah, B.K. Hariprasad accompany Mallikarjun Kharge, who is seeking re-election

title: Medical experts can now volunteer to serve in TTD-run hospitals
content: Under the first-of-its-kind ‘Srivari Vaidya Seva’ pilot initiative, Hindu medical practitioners can enrol themselves through the TTD portal and offer their services

title: Over 2,100 vehicles seized in drive against tampered number plates in Hyderabad
content: Motorists found deliberately altering, concealing or forging registration numbers could face criminal cases, says Hyderabad City Police Commissioner VC Sajjanar

title: Kerala Forest department launches 24/7 call centre, tech platforms for solar fence and case management
content: The initiatives include a Forest Call Centre, an online reporting and monitoring portal for solar fences, and the integration of the (Hostile Activity Watch Kernel) platform for handling forest offence records to courts online through the District Court Management System

title: Dengue: Punalur municipality launches measures on war footing
content: A spike in the vector population was detected during a recent field inspection conducted by the District Vector Control unit

title: Mercury tops 40°C again in Vizag and Anakapalli, Vechalam hottest at 43.89°C
content: Vechalam in Anakapalli is the hottest at 43.89 degrees Celsius; high humidity adds to the discomfort and parents worry about schools reopening on June 8

title: CBSE files police complaint over cyber attacks on post-result portal
content: The CBSE said that in view of the nature, scale, and coordinated character of these attacks, CBSE has approached the IFSO Unit for a detailed investigation and appropriate legal action against those responsible

title: Nicobar port has no ‘strategic goals’, Finance Ministry body said in 2024
content: Centre has cited the ‘strategic’ nature of the proposed ₹81,000-crore Great Nicobar Project for not making public the contents of a report by a High Powered Committee (HPC) on the cumulative environmental impact of the project

title: Nirmala Sitharaman calls for people’s movement to protect environment
content: Participating in the ‘Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam’ plantation programme organised at Chikkajogihalli village in Kudligi taluk on the occasion of World Environment Day, the Finance Minister said the prosperity of humanity depended on preserving nature, wildlife and greenery.

title: Great Nicobar project meant to benefit Adani, it’s not about defence: Rahul Gandhi
content: On World Environment Day, the Opposition leader launches an online petition against the Great Nicobar Island transhipment hub project, urges youngsters to sign up and “choose green over greed”

title: How this ‘tree man’ from Kerala has planted over 20,000 trees in 26 years
content: Palakkad native M. Shyamkumars’ work extends beyond Kerala, with trees planted in Puducherry and Kavaratti

title: The Hindu Huddle 2026 Highlights: Actors Huma Qureshi, Kritika Kamra discuss the global reach of OTT
content: Talking about pay parity between male and female actors in the film industry, Huma Qureshi said that Films are made through the male gaze. “Women are just accessories. The mindset is deeply rooted”

title: CII, MCC to launch ‘Adopt-a-Stretch’ initiative in Mysuru
content: The campaign aims to position Mysuru among the cleanest cities in the country; clean-up drive to be held on Saturday as part of the World Environment Day celebrations

title: ‘Mollywood Times’ movie review: A flawed, bloated exposé of the industry’s dark underbelly
content: Marketed as a “hate letter to cinema,” ‘Mollywood Times’ explores themes of ambition and disillusionment, but struggles with pacing and uneven writing that detract from its potential impact

title: Substantial portions of White Paper on Kerala’s fiscal health AI-generated: Thomas Isaac
content: Former Finance Minister asks Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan to clarify whether the document was indeed AI-generated, and whether sensitive data from the Secret Section of the Finance department was released externally and analysed using such tools

title: Start serious discussions with Union government on worsening Centre-State fiscal relations, urges White Paper on Kerala’s fiscal health
content: Kerala today operates in a less flexible fiscal space with the discontinuation of the Goods and Services Tax  compensation, the limits placed on borrowings and the elimination of revenue deficit grants under the 16th Finance Commission regime, according to the document

title: In pictures: The Hindu Huddle 2026 | Day 1
content: The Hindu Huddle on June 5 and 6, plays host to a host of thought-provoking talks, panel discussions and conversations. Here's a look at how the event unfolded.

title: NAC–A.P. bags two national awards at IINA 2026
content: Academy wins platinum award for skill development initiative; Director General G. Ganesh Kumar receives gold award for excellence in government service

title: Himachal’s green contribution worth ₹90,000 crore inadequately compensated by Centre: CM Sukhu
content: State’s rightful claim would be strongly raised before Central government at upcoming NITI Aayog meeting.

title: DVAC files FIR over cash-for-jobs complaint in Municipal Administration Department during K.N. Nehru’s tenure
content: Madras High Court Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan advise the agency not to precipitate the issue until the next hearing of the case on June 23, 2026

title: Assam Cabinet undergoes expansion, 12 MLAs sworn in
content: Governor Lakshman Prasad Acharya administered the oath of office and secrecy to the MLAs at a function at the Jyoti-Bishnu Antarjatik Kala Mandir in Guwahati

title: Piyush Goyal praises TTD’s AI system for improving pilgrim services at Tirumala
content: After visiting the Integrated Command and Control Centre at the Vaikuntham Queue Complex, the Union Commerce Minister calls the TTD’s AI-driven pilgrim management a model of transparent public service

title: Bhangar blast case, NIA declares ex-TMC MLA Saokat Molla absconding, shares details with BSF
content: ‘There are indications that he may be hiding at a relative's residence. Several teams are carrying out searches and verification exercises. We are confident of tracing him soon,’ an NIA official said

title: Chief Minister charges BRS government with focusing  more on ‘commissions’ not projects
content: Stress of the earlier regime was on electro-mechanical works not on civil works like land acquisition, says Revanth Reddy

title: This year, 1,59,151 engineering seats available in State’s engineering colleges
content: There is a 500-seat increase in government colleges, a 2,580-seat increase in private universities, a 1,695-seat increase in private colleges, and a marginal increase of 60 seats in deemed universities as per the matrix.

title: India-China relations have improved from ‘reset and fresh start’ to ‘new level’: Chinese envoy Xu Feihong
content: Chinese Ambassador to India Xu Feihong in conversation with Suhasini Haidar during the session ‘India-China tango: Asian powers shaping the emerging world order’

title: Here are the big stories from Karnataka today
content: Welcome to the Karnataka Today newsletter, your guide from The Hindu on the major news stories to follow today. Curated and written by Nalme Nachiyar.

title: Government firm on completing the pending irrigation projects in erstwhile Mahabubnagar district, says CM
content: Chief Minister Revanth Reddy and his cabinet colleague Uttam Kumar Reddy launch trenchant attack on KCR and BRS for hoodwinking people

title: Case against relegating Tamil Thai Vazhthu to third place during T.N. CM Vijay’s swearing-in ceremony withdrawn
content: Judges say they cannot entertain the petition sans any challenge to the Union Home Ministry’s circular regarding the playing of the national song and national anthem during events attended by the President/Governors

title: Compensatory afforestation for biodiversity loss in Nicobar a bogus argument: Jairam Ramesh
content: ‘In India, unlike in many other parts of the world, when we protect nature, you also protect culture’

title: Pawan Kalyan launches drive to make 2.5 crore seed balls on World Environment Day
content: The Deputy Chief Minister joins self-help group women and students in making seed balls at Mulapadu, watches drones disperse them, and sets a June 15 target across 300 locations

title: AI no longer a speculative technology but an operational reality: CJI Surya Kant
content: CJI Kant said AI is reshaping governance, commerce, warfare, communication, public administration, and the exercise of judicial and sovereign power itself

title: Draft RMP amendments to ease plot mergers, family sub-division in Bengaluru; real estate to thrive?
content: The proposed move is expected to make the government’s B-to-A Khata conversion scheme more attractive, while contributing to densification of the city

title: Annamalai floats ‘political movement’, to fight next general election in Tamil Nadu
content: The political movement is inspired by the ideals of former President A.P.J. Abdul Kalam. No animosity against the BJP, says Annamalai who is the former president of BJP’s Tamil Nadu unit

title: JEE-Advanced data breach claims misleading, factually incorrect: IIT-Roorkee
content: The institute further asserted that "no sensitive information was compromised or mass-extracted" and that the incident had "zero impact on examination outcomes, including marks, ranks, and category of the candidates"

title: SC balances child’s right to know paternity and alleged father’s right to privacy
content: The top court has consistently held in various judgments that DNA tests should not be ordered routinely, but there has never been a conclusive answer to when they should be permitted; in case of continuous denial of paternity, the court resorts to DNA test in the interest of justice

title: Madras High Court refuses to stay circular mandating private schools in T.N. to display fee structure on notice boards
content: Justice M. Dhandapani rejects the plea for interim stay and says, he would instead take up the main case for final disposal after two weeks

title: PM Modi visits L&T's Hazira facility in Gujarat's Surat, reviews its projects
content: PM Modi visited the AM Naik Heavy Engineering Complex, L&T's state-of-the-art manufacturing facility, and was shown around the facility by the company officials

title: Delhi HC refuses urgent hearing on plea against Cockroach Janta Party protest
content: The petitioner, in his plea, sought deployment of "immediate preventive, regulatory, and crowd-control measures" at the IGI Airport, metro stations, and highway entry points

title: CM Vijay chairs TNSDMA meeting to review southwest monsoon preparedness
content: Following the onset of the southwest monsoon over the South Andaman Sea on May 16, conditions continue to remain favourable for its further intensification

title: Amit Shah says Centre won't tolerate demographic changes in West Bengal, Tripura, Bihar
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah said that the government’s ‘smart border’ project is a new security grid which includes cutting-edge tech, local administration and border sentinels

title: Satellite Data Shows Iran War-Led Slowdown In India's Major Industrial Hubs
content: Remote sensing data analysed through OSINT research by NDTV Datafy shows reduced emissions of nitrogen dioxide (NO2) in at least four industrial clusters.

title: Video: 'Mushroom Cloud' Over Mexico As Tanker Carrying Stolen Fuel Explodes
content: Videos circulating on social media showed the moment the tanker went up in flames near Tepeaca, a small city roughly 35 kilometres east of Puebla.

title: Will Trinamool MLAs Fall In Line? Crucial Meeting At Mamata Banerjee's House
content: The meeting has been called at the Kalighat house of Banerjee. Days before, 60 of the 80 MLAs had skipped the meeting at Banerjee's house.

title: 'In India, We Almost Find A Global Market': Swedish Ambassador To NDTV
content: Ambassador Jan Thesleff, who is set to conclude his posting in India by mid-August, described the visit as transformational, not just for bilateral ties, but for the broader India-Europe strategic axis at a moment of profound global uncertainty.

title: Data Centre Specialist AirTrunk To Invest Rs 3 Lakh Crore In India: PM Modi
content: Leading hyperscale data centre specialist AirTrunk plans to invest more than Rs 3 lakh crore in India by 2030 in support of digital infrastructure capacity, positioning the country as a cornerstone of global growth strategy.

title: Tata Trusts Refute Allegations On NRTT Share Transfer
content: Tata Trusts has rejected allegations surrounding a share transfer executed by the Navajbai Ratan Tata Trust (NRTT) in 1989, calling the claims "baseless" and "malafide"

title: India, US May Execute First Phase Of Trade Pact By Next Month: Piyush Goyal
content: India and the US are moving towards closing all the open ends of the interim trade agreement, and both sides are likely to execute the "very, very vibrant" first phase of the pact by the middle of next month, Piyush Goyal said on Friday.

title: Xi Jinping To Meet Kim Jong Un: Why It Matters And What To Expect
content: China's President Xi Jinping will visit North Korea on Monday as he seeks to cement Beijing's influence in its own backyard.

title: Why Rajya Sabha Ticket For Ex MP Has Madhya Pradesh Congress Nervous
content: On paper, the Congress has enough strength to send Meenakshi Natarajan to the Upper House. But politics rarely follows the script written on paper.

title: Delhi Hotel Owner Helped Bangladeshi Nationals Get Fake Indian Documents
content: Lavkesh Bajaj helped Bangladeshi nationals secure passport Indian identity documents on his residential address in exchange for money.

title: Assam's Moran, Motok Communities Begin Shutdown To Demand Cabinet Inclusion
content: Protesters staged demonstrations at several locations, with one of the major gatherings reported at Rupai Siding-Kakopathar Tini Aali in Tinsukia district.

title: Trump Sees Win As US Senate Approves $70 Billion For Immigration Crackdown
content: The bill would fund ICE and Border Patrol through the rest of Trump's term, handing the Republican leader a major victory on one of his signature issues.

title: Decoding UP's Political Battle: Can BJP Counter Samajwadi's PDA Formula?
content: While Akhilesh Yadav's Samajwadi Party is attempting to move beyond the Muslim-Yadav label through its PDA (Pichhda, Dalit, Alpsankhyak) formula, the BJP too has sharpened its focus on non-Yadav OBCs and other key social groups.

title: India, Norway Seal Green Partnership, Maritime Deals in PM Modi's Oslo Visit
content: In an exclusive interview to NDTV, Norway's Ambassador to India, May-Elin Stener, called the visit "very successful" and pointed to a sweep of concrete outcomes that she said would define the partnership for years to come.

title: "This Is Not 1962": Arunachal Chief Minister Dismisses China's Claim
content: The chief minister said the focus should remain on development, noting that Arunachal Pradesh is witnessing major investments in infrastructure, border connectivity and hydropower projects.

title: $27.6 Billion: Behind Centre's Tax Relief To Foreign Investors On G-Secs
content: Global investors assess countries on several parameters, including yields, tax treatment, liquidity, currency risk, policy stability and ease of exit

title: Putin Offers Su-57 Fighter To Delhi As India Searches Its 5th-Gen Jet
content: The Su-57, developed by Russia's Sukhoi Design Bureau, is designed to carry out a wide range of combat missions, including engagement of air, ground and maritime targets.

title: PM Modi's 12-year Tenure "Phenomenal Victory": Pawan Kalyan To NDTV
content: The Jana Sena Party chief also reaffirmed his unwavering commitment to PM Modi, pledging to stand by him "through thick and thin."

title: US-Iran Live Updates: "Would Be Honoured To Meet": Trump On Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei
content: US-Iran War Live: Iran fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain hours after US conducted strikes on Iran's Qeshm Island amid ongoing peace talks between Washington and Tehran.

title: India leads among immigrant founders of US billion-dollar startups
content: Lubna Kably is a senior editor, who focuses on various policies and legislation. In particular, she writes extensively on immigration and tax policies. The Indian diaspora is the largest in the world; through her articles she demystifies the immigration-policy related developments in select countries for outbound students, job aspirants and employees. She also analyses the impact of Income-tax and GST related developments for individuals and business entities. Read More
